source: PDF source (2024ND72.pdf) shows 'to be resolved are questions of law' in quoted passage from case law standard
evidence: Line 52 in DB contains 'if the only issues to judgment is be resolved are questions of law' within a block quote. The PDF shows the correct quoted text as 'if the only issues to be resolved are questions of law.' This is a clear OCR/transcription error where 'be' was dropped and text was run together with the following word.
note: This error appears in [¶5] within a quoted passage citing the summary judgment standard. The source text clearly shows 'to be resolved' not 'to judgment is be resolved'.

source: PDF source (2024ND72.pdf) shows 'Summary judgment is appropriate' in quoted passage continuing the legal standard
evidence: Line 52 in DB contains 'Summary appropriate if the issues in the case are such that...' within the same block quote. The PDF clearly shows 'Summary judgment is appropriate if the issues...' The words 'judgment is' were dropped during transcription.
note: This error appears immediately after the first error in the same quoted passage. The two errors suggest a transcription/OCR problem in processing this particular block quote from case law.

source: /Users/jerod/refs/nd/opin/pdfs/2024/2024ND68.pdf
evidence: At end of ¶4 in DB: 'remain unresolved. П' (Cyrillic П = U+041F, Latin Letter P). PDF at same location shows: 'remain unresolved.' (period), then blank line, then 'II' (Roman numeral) centered on its own line. The Cyrillic П is a misrecognition of Roman numeral II, and the joined structure violates PDF layout where section marker stands alone.
note: High-risk OCR error: Cyrillic character confused with Roman numeral. Also violates paragraph structure (section marker should be separate block).

source: /tmp/src_2024ND66.txt lines 208-212

source: PDF renders as Roman numeral I; extracted DB text contains Greek iota (U+0399); visual inspection of page 2 confirms Roman numeral usage
